methodology.name: hegemon-formal-blueprint-dag-v1
methodology.summary: LeanMarathon-inspired formal-security blueprint: every release claim is a reviewed graph node with implementation bindings, evidence, dependencies, and cheap falsification cases.

nodes.formal_statement: Every cargo-audit advisory or yanked crate that remains in release code or the formal-core checker lockfile is represented by an explicit non-expired waiver entry with policy metadata.
nodes.informal_argument: The dependency audit gate parses cargo-audit output and the waiver file, counts waived and unwaived findings, and fails if an advisory is not accounted for. The formal-core checker crate is audited separately so the assurance tooling does not bypass the same dependency discipline.

target_review.notes: The claim is deliberately operational: it proves review coverage for known dependency findings, not absence of future advisories.

falsification_cases.description: A cargo-audit finding with no matching waiver must fail the dependency gate.
The formal-core checker lockfile is audited independently from the root workspace lockfile.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node does not claim dependency freshness; it claims enforced waiver accounting for known cargo-audit findings.

nodes.formal_statement: The release `hegemon-node` binary is rejected by the release gate if forbidden classical curve, pairing, RSA, or trusted-setup verifier surfaces are detected in the linked artifact or dependency surface scanned by the policy.
nodes.informal_argument: The binary primitive audit is the release-facing check for the design rule that the native node must stay PQ-clean. It is paired with dependency waiver accounting so both the linked symbols and known package advisories are visible before release.

target_review.notes: The claim is scoped to the audited release node binary and the current forbidden-primitive pattern set.

falsification_cases.description: Forbidden ECC/pairing/RSA/trusted-setup symbols or dependencies must fail the security audit.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node does not prove that every transitive crate is PQ-secure; it enforces the current release binary primitive policy.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction-balance transition, a valid balance witness must have concrete balance slots, the native slot delta must equal `fee - value_balance`, and the stablecoin/non-native slot rules must hold.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_has_slots`, `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_rejects_slot_overflow`, `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_native_delta`, and `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_stablecoin_rules` prove core facts about the executable balance kernel. `gen_transaction_vectors` emits Lean-derived native-fee, value-balance, non-native conservation, counterfeit rejection, stablecoin issuance, stablecoin mismatch, native-asset stablecoin rejection, and balance-slot overflow examples. The formal-core gate runs production `transaction-circuit` conformance against `TransactionWitness::balance_slots` and `TransactionWitness::validate`. The existing TLA+ model remains bounded abstraction evidence for the larger transaction balance/nullifier invariant shape.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for TransactionWitness balance-slot and balance-validation behavior, with the TLA+ model retained as bounded abstraction evidence.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the transaction-balance theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production balance-slot or balance-validation behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Removing `BalanceInvariant` or `NullifierUniqueness` from the model must fail the formal inventory check.
When local model checkers are installed, the wrapper can run TLC/Apalache against the transaction model.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks executable balance-slot and validation semantics only. It does not prove note commitment hashing, Merkle hash-function security, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hashing, full AIR/proof-system soundness, or complete Rust refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean Merkle-path transition, path folding uses position-bit orientation at every level, accepts the computed root when the sibling depth matches, and rejects mismatched depths or roots.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.foldPath_empty`, `Hegemon.Transaction.foldPath_even_step`, `Hegemon.Transaction.foldPath_odd_step`, `Hegemon.Transaction.verifyPath_accepts_computed_root`, `Hegemon.Transaction.verifyPath_rejects_wrong_length`, and `Hegemon.Transaction.verifyPath_rejects_wrong_root` prove the executable Merkle path control-flow facts. `gen_merkle_vectors` emits Lean-derived empty, left/right orientation, high-position, wrong-root, and wrong-length examples. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against the production `MerklePath::root_with` and `MerklePath::verify_with_depth_and_node` helpers, while `MerklePath::verify` delegates to the same fold helper with the real Poseidon2 `merkle_node` combiner.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for Merkle path fold, position-bit orientation, explicit-depth admission, and root equality semantics. Poseidon2 hash security/equivalence remains outside this claim.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the Merkle path orientation/depth/root theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production Merkle path folding, depth admission, or root comparison away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Changing SmallWood native membership validation away from explicit MERKLE_TREE_DEPTH gating or MerklePath::verify must be caught by transaction-circuit regression coverage.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks Merkle path control flow only. It does not prove Poseidon2 merkle_node cryptographic security, Poseidon2 implementation equivalence, note commitment hashing, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hashing, full AIR/proof-system soundness, or complete Rust refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction public-input shape predicate, fixed-width vectors, boolean flags, inactive-field zeroing, active nullifier/commitment nonzero checks, nonempty transaction admission, canonical balance-slot asset ordering, and stablecoin asset membership must hold before verifier-facing public inputs are accepted.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_accepts_valid`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_bad_input_flag`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_inactive_input_nonzero`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_active_input_zero`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_inactive_output_nonzero_commitment`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_empty_transaction`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_bad_slot_zero`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_padding_not_suffix`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_duplicate_asset`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_stablecoin_missing_asset`, and `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_accepts_stablecoin_present` prove representative public-input shape acceptance and rejection facts over the executable predicate. `gen_public_input_vectors` emits Lean-derived malformed flag, inactive nonzero, active zero, empty transaction, balance-slot ordering, and stablecoin-membership examples. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against production `TransactionPublicInputsP3::validate` through the `transaction-circuit` test.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for the TransactionPublicInputsP3 verifier-admission shape. Statement-hash binding and proof-system soundness remain outside this claim.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the public-input shape theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production TransactionPublicInputsP3 shape validation away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate public-input vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks verifier-facing public-input shape admission only. It does not prove Poseidon2 implementation equivalence, note commitment hashing, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hash derivation, statement-hash preimage binding, STARK or SmallWood proof-system soundness, or complete Rust refinement. Statement-hash preimage binding is covered separately by formal.transaction-statement-hash-binding.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction public/serialized input binding predicate, the public transaction fields and serialized STARK public-input fields must agree on merkle root, fee, signed value balance, balance-slot assets, and stablecoin binding payload before verifier-facing TransactionPublicInputsP3 construction succeeds.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputBinding.bindPublicInputs_accepts_valid`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputBinding.validBinding_accepts_stablecoin`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputBinding.validBinding_accepts_omitted_balance_assets`, and the companion rejection theorems prove representative binding accept/reject facts over the executable predicate. `gen_public_input_binding_vectors` emits Lean-derived valid, omitted-balance-assets fallback, merkle-root mismatch, fee mismatch, signed value-balance mismatch, balance-slot asset mismatch/count mismatch, and stablecoin payload mismatch examples. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against production `transaction_public_inputs_p3_from_parts` through the `transaction-circuit` test.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for the public TransactionPublicInputs / SerializedStarkInputs binding seam. Statement-hash preimage binding is covered separately by formal.transaction-statement-hash-binding; proof-system soundness remains outside this claim.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the public-input binding theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production public/serialized input binding away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate public-input binding vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks public/serialized input field binding before verifier public-input construction only. It does not prove Poseidon2 or BLAKE3 implementation equivalence, transaction statement-hash preimage binding, note commitment hashing, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hash derivation, STARK or SmallWood proof-system soundness, or complete Rust refinement. Statement-hash preimage binding is covered separately by formal.transaction-statement-hash-binding.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction statement-hash preimage grammar, transaction receipts and consensus tx-leaf binding must commit to the same byte layout: domain separator, merkle root, padded nullifier/commitment/ciphertext-hash lists, little-endian scalar fields, raw stablecoin flag byte, and two's-complement signed i128 balances.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.StatementHash.statementPreimage_accepts_valid`, `Hegemon.Transaction.StatementHash.statementPreimage_valid_length`, `Hegemon.Transaction.StatementHash.statementPreimage_accepts_padded_vectors`, `Hegemon.Transaction.StatementHash.statementPreimage_accepts_stablecoin`, and the companion rejection theorems prove representative executable facts for the statement-hash preimage grammar. `gen_statement_hash_vectors` emits Lean-derived exact preimage bytes for full-width, padded, and stablecoin cases plus rejection examples. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against the shared Rust `transaction_statement_preimage_from_parts` and `transaction_statement_hash_from_parts` helpers used by transaction receipts and consensus tx-leaf binding.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for statement-hash byte preimage binding. BLAKE3 cryptographic security, note/ciphertext derivation, and proof-system soundness remain outside this claim.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the statement-hash preimage theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production statement-hash preimage byte order, padding, signed integer encoding, or digest input away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate statement-hash vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks the transaction statement-hash byte preimage grammar and Rust digest call over that preimage only. It does not prove BLAKE3 collision resistance, Poseidon2 implementation equivalence, note commitment hashing, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hash derivation, STARK or SmallWood proof-system soundness, or complete Rust refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean nullifier-state transition, zero nullifiers cannot be staged or imported, successful staging prevents staging the same nullifier again, and successful import prevents both re-importing and re-staging the same nullifier.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Shielded.stage_rejects_zero`, `Hegemon.Shielded.import_rejects_zero`, `Hegemon.Shielded.stage_prevents_duplicate_pending`, `Hegemon.Shielded.import_prevents_reimport`, and `Hegemon.Shielded.import_prevents_restaging` prove the anti-double-spend state behavior over the executable nullifier kernel. `gen_shielded_vectors` emits Lean-derived nullifier examples, and the formal-core gate runs the production `protocol-shielded-pool` conformance test against them. The native node uses that shared helper for mempool staging, block validation, state replay, and author preview.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for protocol-shielded-pool nullifier stage/import behavior.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the nullifier-state theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production nullifier stage/import behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node does not prove full transaction balance conservation, note commitment correctness, or proof-system soundness.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean fork-choice transition, a candidate tip is selected over the current tip exactly when it has greater work, or equal work with greater height, or equal work and height with a lexicographically lower hash.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.higher_work_wins`, `Hegemon.Consensus.lower_work_loses`, `Hegemon.Consensus.equal_work_higher_height_wins`, `Hegemon.Consensus.equal_work_lower_height_loses`, `Hegemon.Consensus.equal_work_height_uses_hash_order`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.same_tip_not_better` prove deterministic two-tip ordering over the executable fork-choice kernel. `gen_consensus_vectors` emits Lean-derived fork-choice examples, and the formal-core gate runs the production `consensus::fork_choice` conformance test against them. `PowConsensus` and native block import both call that helper. The existing TLA+ model remains the bounded aggregate-work/finality abstraction.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for the deterministic fork-choice ordering helper, with the TLA+ finality model retained as bounded abstraction evidence.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the fork-choice ordering theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production fork-choice ordering away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Removing `ForkChoiceInvariant` or `FinalityInvariant` from the model must fail the formal inventory check.
When local model checkers are installed, the wrapper can run TLC/Apalache against the PoW model.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves deterministic two-tip ordering only. It does not prove PoW admission arithmetic, SHA-256 header-hash implementation equivalence, every network partition, timestamp edge case, or long-range adversarial mining schedule.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean PoW admission transition, a block candidate is rejected when height, pow_bits, timestamp, compact target, hash threshold, claimed cumulative Work48, or Work48 overflow violate the specified arithmetic policy; otherwise the expected cumulative work is accepted.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.compactTarget_rejects_zero_mantissa`, `Hegemon.Consensus.compactTarget_rejects_large_exponent`, `Hegemon.Consensus.timestamp_rejects_parent_equal`, `Hegemon.Consensus.timestamp_rejects_median_equal`, `Hegemon.Consensus.timestamp_rejects_future_skew`, `Hegemon.Consensus.checkedWorkAdd_ok`, `Hegemon.Consensus.checkedWorkAdd_rejects_overflow`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powAdmission_rejects_height`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powAdmission_rejects_pow_bits`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powAdmission_rejects_insufficient_work`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.powAdmission_accepts_valid` prove core facts about the executable PoW admission table. `gen_pow_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against the production `evaluate_pow_admission` helper used by `PowConsensus` plus the `consensus-light-client` compact-target, hash-threshold, and Work48 cumulative-work helpers used by native import and bridge verification.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for PoW target, timestamp, hash-threshold, and Work48 cumulative-work admission semantics.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the PoW admission theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production consensus or light-client PoW arithmetic away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Existing PoW rule tests must continue rejecting target, timestamp, and admission-arithmetic violations.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks arithmetic admission over already-computed PoW hashes. It does not prove SHA-256 implementation equivalence, retarget economics, network orphan policy, or long-range finality. Header preimage serialization is covered separately by consensus.header-preimage-binding.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean header grammar, the signing preimage is the ASCII domain `block` followed by the fixed consensus header fields in the specified little-endian and byte-array order, and is independent of signature, BFT bitmap, and PoW seal payloads; the full header preimage appends signature length/payload, optional bitmap tag/length/payload, and optional PoW seal tag/nonce/pow_bits.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.Header.signingPreimage_independent_of_auth_payloads`, `Hegemon.Consensus.Header.sample_signing_preimage_length`, `Hegemon.Consensus.Header.sample_pow_full_header_length`, `Hegemon.Consensus.Header.bft_full_header_length`, `Hegemon.Consensus.Header.unsigned_full_header_length`, `Hegemon.Consensus.Header.pow_header_has_pow_tag`, `Hegemon.Consensus.Header.bft_header_has_bitmap_tag`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.Header.unsigned_header_has_absent_auth_tags` prove executable facts about the header preimage grammar. `gen_header_vectors` emits Lean-derived exact byte preimages, and the formal-core gate checks them against production `BlockHeader::signing_preimage_v1` and `BlockHeader::full_header_preimage_v1`, the helpers used by miner signature verification and PoW work hashing.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for consensus header signing and full-header work-hash preimage grammar. SHA-256 security and implementation equivalence remain outside this node.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the header preimage theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production header preimage serialization away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The full formal-core gate must run the header preimage conformance check with generated Lean vectors.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks consensus header preimage byte grammar only. It does not prove SHA-256 collision resistance, SHA-256 implementation equivalence, ML-DSA unforgeability, semantic correctness of header field values, retarget economics, network orphan policy, or long-range finality.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean PoW miner-identity transition, a header can be accepted only if it does not carry a BFT signature bitmap, its validator-set commitment resolves to a registered miner key, its signature payload has exactly the ML-DSA-65 signature byte length, signature byte parsing succeeds, and signature verification succeeds.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.powMinerIdentity_accepts_valid`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powMinerIdentity_rejects_signature_bitmap`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powMinerIdentity_rejects_unregistered`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powMinerIdentity_rejects_bad_signature_length`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powMinerIdentity_rejects_bad_signature_bytes`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.powMinerIdentity_rejects_failed_signature_verification` prove core facts about the executable PoW miner-identity admission table. `gen_miner_identity_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against the production `evaluate_pow_miner_identity` helper used by `PowConsensus::verify_pow_miner_signature` after real ML-DSA parsing and verification.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for PoW miner identity and signature admission ordering. ML-DSA cryptographic security and implementation correctness remain outside this node.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the miner-identity theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production miner-identity admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Existing PoW rule tests must continue rejecting unregistered miners, malformed miner signatures, and forbidden BFT signature bitmaps.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks miner identity and signature admission ordering only. It does not prove ML-DSA unforgeability, ML-DSA implementation equivalence, validator-set governance, miner key custody, retarget economics, network orphan policy, or long-range finality. Header signing-hash byte grammar is covered separately by consensus.header-preimage-binding.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean version-policy transition, initial version bindings are allowed, activation events add bindings at and after their activation height, retirement events remove bindings at and after their retirement height, retirement wins over same-height activation, duplicate initial bindings are deduplicated, and block admission reports the first unsupported transaction version in transaction order.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.versionPolicy_initial_accepts`, `Hegemon.Consensus.versionPolicy_rejects_unknown_initial`, `Hegemon.Consensus.versionPolicy_rejects_before_activation`, `Hegemon.Consensus.versionPolicy_accepts_at_activation`, `Hegemon.Consensus.versionPolicy_accepts_before_retirement`, `Hegemon.Consensus.versionPolicy_rejects_at_retirement`, `Hegemon.Consensus.versionPolicy_retirement_wins_same_height`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.versionPolicy_reports_first_unsupported_in_tx_order` prove representative executable facts about the version schedule admission table. `gen_version_policy_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against the production `VersionSchedule::validate_versions` helper used by both `PowConsensus::apply_block` and `BftConsensus::apply_block`.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for consensus version admission. Protocol governance and per-version proof soundness remain outside this node.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the version-policy theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production version-schedule admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The existing consensus integration test must continue rejecting unsupported versions before activation and accepting them at activation.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks schedule admission semantics only. It does not prove social upgrade governance, protocol manifest correctness, proof-system soundness for each version, transaction-proof backend security, or full native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean header-MMR opening-shape transition, valid openings must have a matching context, in-range leaf index, peak count equal to the leaf-count peak decomposition, sibling count equal to the selected peak height, and path orientation bits derived from the leaf's local index.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmr.peak_ranges_six`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmr.valid_shape_accepts`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmr.singleton_shape_accepts`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmr.four_leaf_left_orientation`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmr.four_leaf_right_orientation`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmr.rejects_context_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmr.rejects_leaf_out_of_range`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmr.rejects_empty_leaf_set`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmr.rejects_peak_count_mismatch`, and `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmr.rejects_sibling_count_mismatch` prove representative facts about the MMR opening shape table. `gen_bridge_header_mmr_vectors` emits Lean-derived accept/reject examples, and the formal-core gate checks those vectors against `evaluate_header_mmr_opening_shape`, the production helper used by `verify_header_mmr_opening` and the long-range verifier's shared MMR context path.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for header MMR opening shape and path orientation. SHA-256/MMR hash binding and FlyClient sampling soundness remain outside this node.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the header MMR opening-shape theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production MMR opening shape admission or path orientation away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate header MMR vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks header MMR opening shape and left/right path orientation over already-computed hashes only. It does not prove SHA-256 implementation equivalence, MMR parent/root hash security, FlyClient sampling soundness, bridge message-root hashing, external-chain verifier behavior, or complete long-range light-client soundness. Header MMR parent/root transcript byte grammar is covered separately by bridge.header-mmr-transcript-binding.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean header-MMR transcript transition, parent hash preimages must bind the exact node domain, u32 little-endian level, and ordered left/right child hashes; root hash preimages must bind the exact root domain, u64 little-endian leaf count, u32 little-endian peak count, and ordered peak hash sequence.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.parent_domain_bytes`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.root_domain_bytes`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.sample_parent_length`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.sample_parent_hex`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.max_level_parent_hex`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.empty_root_length`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.empty_root_hex`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.two_peak_root_length`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.two_peak_root_hex`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.reversed_two_peak_root_hex`, and `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.root_order_binds_peak_sequence` prove representative byte-layout facts about the parent/root transcript grammar. `gen_bridge_header_mmr_transcript_vectors` emits Lean-derived parent and root preimage cases, and the formal-core gate checks those vectors against `header_mmr_parent_preimage_v1` and `header_mmr_root_preimage_v1`, the production helpers hashed by header-MMR construction and verification.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for header MMR parent/root transcript byte grammar. BLAKE3 security/equivalence and full MMR hash binding remain outside this node.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the header MMR transcript theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing production header MMR parent/root transcript bytes, endian encodings, left/right order, peak count, or peak order away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate header MMR transcript vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks header MMR parent/root hash preimage grammar only. It does not prove BLAKE3 cryptographic security or implementation equivalence, SHA-256 header-hash implementation equivalence, MMR collision resistance, full MMR hash binding, FlyClient sampling soundness, external-chain verifier behavior, or complete long-range light-client soundness.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean FlyClient sampler transition, deterministic sample derivation must bind the exact domain-separated transcript bytes, little-endian range bounds and sample counter, reduce the digest prefix modulo the sample span, preserve duplicate samples, truncate to the requested sample count, and return no samples for empty or reversed ranges.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Bridge.FlyClient.sample_domain_bytes`, `Hegemon.Bridge.FlyClient.valid_transcript_length`, `Hegemon.Bridge.FlyClient.valid_transcript_hex`, `Hegemon.Bridge.FlyClient.max_index_transcript_hex`, `Hegemon.Bridge.FlyClient.sample_height_modulo`, `Hegemon.Bridge.FlyClient.sample_height_large_prefix`, `Hegemon.Bridge.FlyClient.sample_heights_allow_duplicates`, `Hegemon.Bridge.FlyClient.sample_heights_truncate_to_sample_count`, and the companion empty-range theorems prove representative facts about the sample transcript and index arithmetic. `gen_bridge_flyclient_vectors` emits Lean-derived transcript and digest-prefix examples, and the formal-core gate checks those vectors against `flyclient_sample_transcript_preimage_v1`, `flyclient_sample_height_from_digest_prefix`, and `flyclient_sample_indices_from_digest_prefixes`, the helpers used by `flyclient_sample_indices` before long-range sample openings are verified.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for FlyClient sample transcript bytes and digest-prefix reduction. BLAKE3 security/equivalence and probabilistic sampling soundness remain outside this node.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the FlyClient sampler transcript/index theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing production FlyClient transcript bytes, little-endian counter encoding, modulo reduction, duplicate handling, or empty-range handling away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate FlyClient sampler vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks deterministic FlyClient sample transcript and digest-prefix arithmetic only. It does not prove BLAKE3 cryptographic security or implementation equivalence, MMR hash binding, PoW hash security, probabilistic FlyClient sampling soundness, external-chain verifier behavior, or complete long-range light-client soundness.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean checkpoint-output transition, canonical BridgeCheckpointOutputV1 bytes must equal the exact 35-byte output domain followed by the fixed 404-byte journal tuple, with source/rules/header/work/message fields in protocol order and u64/u128/u32 scalars encoded little-endian.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native derived block commitment admission. Scope is equality of already-derived roots, counts, header history, and checked supply digest.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the block commitment-admission theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing the production block commitment-admission table away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Native announced-block import must reject internally consistent PoW headers whose claimed body commitment fields do not match derived roots/counts/supply.
Native branch replay must reject persisted block records whose bridge message commitment no longer matches decoded actions.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks the native block derived commitment-admission table only. It does not prove commitment-tree hash security, BLAKE3 bridge message-root security or implementation equivalence, header-MMR hash binding, PoW threshold validity, full action payload validation, tx proof soundness, supply economics, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean message-root transcript function, valid 48-byte message hashes are encoded as domain || len(count-bytes) || count || len(hash) || hash..., invalid hash lengths are rejected before the Rust root helper can be called, and ordered pairs produce different transcripts when reversed.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean `Hegemon.Bridge.MessageRoot` kernel fixes the byte grammar that feeds bridge `message_root` hashing. `gen_bridge_vectors` emits empty, single, ordered-pair, reversed-pair, and invalid hash-length cases, and the production `protocol-kernel` conformance test checks the Rust root preimage helper against those Lean bytes. The independent bridge reference vectors continue to rederive the BLAKE3 XOF-48 payload hash, canonical message hash, single-message root, and inbound replay key without importing production bridge helpers.

target_review.notes: The target is accepted for Lean-backed transcript grammar and independent BLAKE3 reference vectors. Larger bridge light-client and external-chain proof systems remain out of scope.

falsification_cases.description: Changing the Rust bridge message-root transcript domain, count bytes, length prefixes, or ordered hash sequence must fail the Lean-generated conformance test.
Changing payload bytes or expected payload hash must fail vector verification.
Replay keys must differ across source chain ids and nonces.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node covers bridge message-root transcript grammar, canonical vector derivations, and replay-key vector checks. It does not prove BLAKE3 cryptographic security or implementation equivalence, every bridge light-client proof system, or external-chain covenant behavior.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean replay-state transition, successful staging prevents staging the same key again, and successful import prevents both re-importing and re-staging the same key.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Bridge.stage_prevents_duplicate_pending`, `Hegemon.Bridge.import_prevents_reimport`, and `Hegemon.Bridge.import_prevents_restaging` prove the no-duplicate properties over the executable replay-state kernel. `gen_bridge_vectors` emits Lean-derived bridge encoding and replay examples, and the formal-core gate runs the production `protocol-kernel` conformance test against them.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for protocol-kernel bridge encoding and replay helper behavior.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the replay-state theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production bridge encoding or replay helper behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node does not prove BLAKE3 replay-key derivation, bridge light-client validity, or full native-node equivalence beyond the node's use of the protocol-kernel replay helper.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native tx-leaf artifact parser, valid bounded artifact bytes are accepted with the expected summary, missing backend bytes default according to the tx version/default backend rule, and malformed backend IDs, trailing bytes, over-cap counts, over-cap proof lengths, over-cap commitment rows/coefficients, and truncation are rejected.
nodes.informal_argument: Native tx-leaf artifacts are a proof-system trust boundary before consensus consumes receipt metadata. The Lean parser models the exact byte grammar and count caps used by the production decoder. `gen_native_tx_leaf_artifact_vectors` emits accepted and rejected artifacts, and the superneo-hegemon conformance test checks production decoding and canonical re-encoding against those Lean-derived cases.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native tx-leaf artifact parser grammar and canonical re-encoding behavior.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the native tx-leaf artifact parser theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production native tx-leaf artifact decoding or canonical re-encoding away from the Lean executable parser must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native tx-leaf artifact byte grammar, count caps, backend-byte defaulting, trailing-byte rejection, and canonical re-encoding behavior only. It does not prove lattice backend security, STARK proof soundness, receipt-root fold soundness, statement-digest collision resistance, or full native backend cryptographic acceptance.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native receipt-root parser and structural gate, valid bounded artifact bytes parse with the expected summary, non-empty expected leaf sets must match artifact leaf count, fold_count must equal leaf_count - 1, each fold must carry exactly five challenges and exact 11 by 54 parent-row shape, and malformed counts, trailing bytes, and truncation are rejected.
nodes.informal_argument: Native receipt-root artifacts are consumed after tx-leaf verification and before backend fold verification. The Lean parser models the bounded byte grammar, and the Lean schedule predicate models the cheap production validator now called by both native receipt-root verifier entry points before cryptographic fold checks. `gen_native_receipt_root_vectors` emits accepted parser/schedule cases and rejected mismatch cases, and the superneo-hegemon conformance test checks production decoding, canonical re-encoding, and structural-gate agreement.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native receipt-root parser grammar and structural fold-schedule gating. Scope excludes cryptographic fold soundness.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the native receipt-root parser/schedule theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production native receipt-root decoding, canonical re-encoding, or structural scheduling away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native receipt-root byte grammar and cheap structural fold-schedule gating only. It does not prove lattice backend security, backend transcript challenge derivation, fold proof soundness, statement-digest collision resistance, or full native backend cryptographic acceptance.

nodes.formal_statement: The native backend reference-vector verifier accepts committed valid tx-leaf and receipt-root vectors and rejects malformed companions for spec digest, parameter fingerprint, proof digest, STARK proof, and trailing-byte failures.
nodes.informal_argument: Native proof artifacts are a trust boundary. The reference verifier is run by the formal-core gate to ensure canonical decoding and key binding checks keep rejecting known invalid variants.

target_review.notes: The target is accepted as a vector and byte-grammar claim, not as a cryptographic proof of the candidate backend.

falsification_cases.description: The vector bundle includes invalid cases that must continue to be rejected by the reference verifier.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node does not certify the lattice backend's security assumptions; it checks artifact canonicality and known invalid-vector rejection.

nodes.formal_statement: The release node must fail closed for inbound RISC Zero bridge receipts after syntactic and message-binding prechecks until a PQ-clean verifier is available.
nodes.informal_argument: RISC Zero bridge receipts remain offline experimental artifacts because the verifier path can pull classical Groth16/BN254 code into the binary. The safer release behavior is to parse and bind-check enough to reject malformed messages, then reject receipt acceptance.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a fail-closed residual-risk node; it is not production-eligible bridge receipt acceptance.

falsification_cases.description: Inbound bridge message-binding tampering must be rejected by node tests.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node explicitly does not permit inbound RISC Zero receipt acceptance on the release node.

nodes.formal_statement: The active native lattice backend remains labeled candidate-under-review and structural-candidate unless an accepted external-review artifact deliberately changes the release posture.
nodes.informal_argument: The repo contains theorem-grade prose, reference vectors, and a reproducible review package, but it does not yet contain machine-checked Lean proofs or accepted external-review artifacts for production cryptographic acceptance. The posture script keeps the candidate label in force.
